title: EG-3DVG: Expression and Geometry Aware Grounding Decoder for 3D Visual Grounding
abstract: We address three challenges in 3D visual grounding: cross-modal misalignment, intra-class confusion arising from insufficient understanding of fine-grained expression cues, and geometric reasoning errors caused by inaccurate aggregation of spatially relevant visual features. We propose EG-3DVG, a unified framework that addresses these issues through an expression and geometry aware grounding decoder integrating two attention modules: position-guided expression cross-attention (PECA) and geometry-aware masked attention (GMA). We also introduce expression-aware contrastive learning (ECL) to strengthen alignment between target object tokens and expression-relevant words. Experiments on ScanRefer and SR3D/NR3D benchmarks show state-of-the-art performance in 3D bounding box localization and mask prediction.
note: CVPR 2026 accepted paper (poster). No arXiv preprint found. PDF requires CVPR registration to access (https://cvpr.thecvf.com/virtual/2026/poster/40250). Official GitHub repo: https://github.com/Gwan9Wook/EG3DVG (placeholder only as of June 2026, README only, no code released yet). Conference takes place June 5, 2026; paper PDF likely to become publicly available after the conference.
